mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity sell-offs that depress S&P 500 prices over the following week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit availability and falling housing starts indicates a shift from housing-driven to consumption-driven growth, supporting corporate revenue expectations and lifting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the inflation rate by a widening margin, real borrowing costs rise sharply, crushing equity risk premiums and forcing multiple compression that drags down S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations where the real rate spread widens beyond the trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real GDP indicates underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 in a backtest spanning 2000-2020.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain disruptions or demand collapse, prompting profit warnings and equity de-rating over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month rolling mean
failure_condition: The signal yields a positive predictive value below 45% when tested against out-of-sample 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in non-farm payrolls confirm a labor market breakdown, which historically precedes equity bear markets and depresses S&P 500 valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 50% of confirmed labor market breakdown episodes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in new housing starts combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates a deleveraging shock that drains aggregate demand, triggering a risk-off equity sell-off over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: The trigger produces positive or flat 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical instances.
